transactions.description: feat(api): server-side layout persistence + localStorage sync
transactions.detail:

Sync strategy:
- localStorage is primary (instant, offline-capable)
- Server is backup: every savePrefs() also PUTs to server (fire-and-forget)
- On first load when localStorage is empty, falls back to server GET
- Server data backfills localStorage for future instant loads

transactions.description: fix(scrub): strip SonarQube badge lines before generic domain replace
transactions.detail:
Bug: public README on GitHub had four dead SonarQube badge links
pointing at https://sonar.example.com/... — the generic replace rule
`sonar.grosswig-it.de -> sonar.example.com` ran BEFORE the
`/grosswig-it.de/d` delete rule, so the delete rule no longer matched
the already-rewritten lines. Result: dead badges in the public mirror.

Fix: add two explicit delete patterns in the markdown-only loop,
matching any SonarQube project_badges URL by pattern (so later
additions like ci.example.com/sonar also get stripped, and the
already-rewritten sonar.example.com lines are cleaned up):

  -e '/sonar\..*api\/project_badges/d'
  -e '/sonar\.example\.com/d'

The first pattern catches lines that still have an internal sonar host
(covers any future hostname), the second sweeps already-rewritten lines
if the generic replace rule ran first. Defense in depth — neither rule
alone would catch all paths through the script.

Internal READMEs in Gitea still show the live SonarQube badges; only
the public mirror strips them. Tested locally against the current
README.md before commit.

Refs #171 (release-notes generator improvements — scrub edge cases
belong in the same family of post-processing quality issues).

transactions.description: ci(mirror): split release body on <!--INTERNAL--> marker for public mirror
transactions.detail:
The Gitea→GitHub release mirror previously copied the entire release body
verbatim, only scrubbing internal hostnames. That works for URLs but not
for internal terminology (SonarQube, Gitea, Woodpecker, Quality Gate
references etc.) that should never reach the public mirror.

New behavior: if the Gitea release body contains the marker
`<!--INTERNAL-->`, only the part BEFORE the marker is mirrored to GitHub.
Everything after the marker stays in Gitea only. Backward-compatible —
bodies without the marker are treated as fully public.

Usage:

```markdown
## v1.2.0 — public summary
... user-facing changes ...

<!--INTERNAL-->

## Internal release notes
... SonarQube, Gitea, Woodpecker, internal infra ...
```

The mirror picks the first section for the GitHub release; both sections
stay visible in the Gitea release for the team.

URL scrubbing for the public part is preserved as defense-in-depth even
though the public section should not contain internal URLs by convention.

Refs #171 (release-notes generator improvements track this kind of
public/internal split as future work in the generator itself).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

transactions.detail:
Community Branch Plugin is installed, so non-main branches can now be
tracked as separate branches in SonarQube. Without sonar.branch.name,
any manual scan on a feat/* or develop branch would overwrite main's
analysis — bad. With the branch parameter, each branch gets its own
analysis state and new-code period.

- sonar-scan step: if CI_COMMIT_BRANCH != main, append
  -Dsonar.branch.name=$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH
- quality-gate step: fetch project_status with ?branch= param when
  scanning non-main, so the QG check hits the right branch result
- main branch scan behavior unchanged (no branch param)

Needed so develop can get its own Sonar baseline before merging PRs
against it — avoids PR analyses falling back to main as the baseline.

transactions.description: fix(user-profile): summary version uses newest semver, not max-count
transactions.detail:
findLatestDayTopEntries() picked the version with the highest call
count on the most recent active day. On an upgrade day (e.g. Claude
Code 2.1.92 → 2.1.96 mid-session), the older version still dominated
the volume, so User Profile summary showed 2.1.92 even though the
user was actively running 2.1.96 with 46 calls already logged.

Fix: sort versions by semver descending and pick the highest with
non-zero calls. Entrypoint stays on max-count because there's no
natural ordering for entrypoint labels (cli/vscode/intellij/web).

Repro: today 2026-04-09, versions: [2.1.92: N calls, 2.1.96: 46 calls]
with N > 46. Before: summary shows "2.1.92". After: summary shows
"2.1.96".

Uses the existing semverCmpDesc() helper already used by the Version
Health chart, so behavior is consistent across the User Profile
section.

transactions.description: feat(charts): ECharts Phase 1 PoC — Efficiency Trend (#166)
transactions.detail:
Replace Chart.js-based Efficiency Trend with Apache ECharts
implementation: 3 Small Multiples (JSONL/Proxy Ratio, Visible Tokens/1%,
Cache Miss %) + Heatmap Matrix overview above.

This is the Phase 1 Proof of Concept for #166 (chart library
consolidation to Apache ECharts). Rationale: Chart.js cannot cleanly
render 3+ metrics with different scales on a single chart — the
previous triple-axis stacked layout caused visual artifacts (bars
appearing to hang mid-chart because y1's zero-line was physically
mid-canvas). The right fix is separate charts per concern (small
multiples) plus a unified overview chart (heatmap matrix), which needs
a chart library with native heatmap support.

Changes:
- tpl/dashboard.html: add echarts@5.5.1 via jsdelivr CDN alongside
  existing chart.js and google charts loader; replace single canvas
  with 4 div containers (heatmap + 3 small multiples in a CSS grid).
- public/js/dashboard.client.js:
  - New helpers: __effNormalizeRow, __effHeatmapOption,
    __effSmallMultipleOption, __effInitOrSet, __effConnectCharts,
    __effResizeAll.
  - Heatmap Matrix: metric x day grid, per-row min-max normalized to
    0-1, 5-stop color gradient (blue → purple → orange → red). Cell
    labels show raw values with unit-aware formatting (x/K/%).
    Coverage % added as 4th metric row.
  - Small multiple 1 (ratio): line chart with ArkNill B8 baseline
    2.87x as dashed markLine reference.
  - Small multiple 2 (vispct): bar chart with coverage-aware tooltip
    (shows Δq5, sample count, coverage %, low-coverage warning).
  - Small multiple 3 (cachemiss): dashed line chart with auto-scale
    (no more 0-100 forced range that hid 1-2% values).
  - Tooltips synced across 3 small multiples via echarts.connect().
  - Window resize handler calls chart.resize() on all 4 instances with
    120ms debounce.
- Old Chart.js implementation removed (~110 lines including the
  stacked-axis workaround config).

Validation points for this PoC (will be tested on dev deploy):
1. No flickering on window resize / tab switch / data update
2. No devicePixelRatio issues on HDPI Windows
3. No ownerDocument-null crashes on DOM re-layout
4. Smooth data updates via setOption() without re-instantiation

If PoC passes, Phase 2 (progressive migration of remaining 29 Chart.js
+ 2 Google Charts instances) proceeds in dedicated branches. If it
fails, only 4 charts to roll back.

See #166 for the full migration roadmap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

transactions.description: refactor+fix: CC reduction for SonarQube QG + tokens/1% cumulative q5
transactions.detail:
Refactor (resolves 4 CRITICAL CC violations in #165):
- __budgetBuildSankeyRows (CC 141→~2): split budget/api/user branches
  into __budgetRowsBudget / __budgetRowsApi / __budgetRowsUser + shared
  helpers __budgetExpandHosts / __budgetPushLeafs / __budgetPushFinalLeafs
  / __budgetHostSrc / __budgetTopWeights. Pure structural, no behavior
  change.
- renderBudgetEfficiency (CC 28→~6): extract __budgetHandleEmpty,
  __budgetDisposeCharts, __budgetMetricsFromTot, __budgetFillSummary,
  __budgetRenderFuel, __budgetRenderAlert, __budgetAlertParts.
- aggregateHourlyLatency (CC 20→~12): extract __aggAddHourLatency.
- buildReleaseStabilityData (CC 16→~10): extract __releaseBuildOne and
  __releaseStabilityOf.

Fix (Efficiency Trend visible_tokens_per_pct — correctness):
The previous formula divided full-day visible tokens by the LAST
rate_limit snapshot's 5h utilization — mixing a calendar-day aggregate
with a rolling-5h-window point value, giving meaningless ratios (2.4K/1%
while ArkNill baseline is 1.5-2.1M/1%).

New approach:
- Server: collect q5 samples per request into dd.q5_samples (ts, q5,
  tokens). Add computeQ5Consumption() which sorts chronologically and
  sums only positive q5 deltas as "active consumption". Expose new
  fields on proxy_days: q5_consumed_pct, q5_samples,
  proxy_active_visible_tokens, visible_tokens_per_pct_method.
- Client: buildEfficiencyData tracks proxy coverage per day
  (proxy_active_visible_tokens / jsonl visible input+output). Tooltip
  afterLabel shows Δq5, sample count, coverage %, and warns when
  coverage < 50% ("metric understates real value"). Cache Miss axis
  suggestedMax reduced from 100 to 10 to match actual values.

Known limitation: during rollback phases of the 5h window, tokens are
not attributed (delta skipped), so the metric is a lower bound. Proxy
coverage in practice is 25-40% of JSONL visible traffic, so displayed
values are biased low by the coverage ratio — future work should adjust
with coverage factor or clamp chart to days with meaningful proxy data.

README (en/de/ko): add language badges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
